The #1 error (BadRequestError - 16 occurrences) is caused by attempting to use tools with models that don't support them (codestral:22b). Adding a model capability detection layer before tool usage will prevent these errors by dynamically checking if the selected model supports the requested features.

The #1 error pattern (BadRequestError - 16 occurrences) is caused by attempting to use tools with models that don't support them. A code-level model capability detection and fallback mechanism will prevent these errors proactively, improving self-heal rate and reducing error rate.

Creating a comprehensive skill for model tool support detection and graceful degradation will reduce the #1 error pattern (BadRequestError - 16x) by enabling agents to select appropriate models or fall back to non-tool modes, improving task success rate and reducing retry cycles.

Fixing the circular import in handle_task.py will eliminate 7 ImportError occurrences and improve system reliability, since this is a concrete code-level issue with clear symptoms and a known solution pattern exists in the research insights (circular_import_resolution_patterns skill).

Equip the team with proven circular import resolution patterns to fix the recurring handle_task:ImportError (7 occurrences) and prevent future occurrences. This domain knowledge enables engineers to apply the right pattern (lazy import, dependency inversion, code restructuring) based on context, reducing runtime errors and improving code maintainability.

The system is experiencing recurring 'RuntimeError: Task execution failed: Error code: 402' (Insufficient credits) from the OpenRouter API. Implementing a graceful fallback mechanism that detects 402 errors and automatically switches to a secondary provider or a lower-cost backup model will prevent task failures and improve the self-heal rate.

Implementing a robust retry mechanism with exponential backoff specifically for APIConnectionError and ConnectionError in the research and coding tools will reduce the overall error rate and improve task success stability.

The system is experiencing recurring 402 'Insufficient credits' errors across multiple agents (pim, coding), which are currently treated as generic RuntimeErrors. Implementing a specific interceptor for 402 errors allows the system to fail gracefully or alert the user instead of triggering useless self-healing loops that waste further tokens.
